info.description: Container-scoped firewall control plane. Authentication is bearer-token on TCP listeners and peer-credentials on unix sockets.
info.title: Firefik API

get.description: Proxies GET /v1/approvals on the configured control-plane.

post.description: Proxies POST /v1/approvals on the configured control-plane.

get.description: Proxies GET /v1/approvals/{id} on the configured control-plane.

post.description: Proxies POST /v1/approvals/{id}/approve on the configured control-plane.
Proxies POST /v1/approvals/{id}/reject on the configured control-plane.

get.description: Returns the most recent audit events kept in the in-memory ring buffer. Populated only when `history` is included in `FIREFIK_AUDIT_SINK`. Supports `?limit=<n>` and `?since=<RFC3339>`.

parameters.description: RFC3339 timestamp; only events strictly after this are returned
parameters.name: since

get.description: Returns observe-mode proposals per container. Populated only when `FIREFIK_AUTOGEN_MODE=observe`.

post.description: Materialises a pending observe-mode proposal as labels or a policy snippet and records the approval.
Marks an observe-mode proposal as rejected with an optional reason.

get.description: Returns every container visible to the Docker daemon with its parsed firewall configuration and live apply status.

post.description: Accepts up to 100 `{id, action}` entries and runs them against the engine. The endpoint is idempotent per-entry: a successful apply/disable repeated is a no-op. Failures are isolated per-entry.

get.description: Resolves by full container ID or unambiguous prefix. Returns the parsed label-based config merged with the file-rules overlay.

parameters.description: container ID or unambiguous prefix (12-64 hex chars)
parameters.name: id

post.description: Re-reads labels + file-rules and installs the merged rule-sets. Audit source = `api`.
post.summary: Apply firewall rules to a container

post.description: Removes kernel chains + any associated audit entries for the container. Audit source = `api`.
post.summary: Disable firewall rules for a container

get.description: Returns the set of compiled policies keyed by name.

post.description: Parses + compiles `dsl` in the request body and returns `{ok, errors, warnings}` without touching state.

get.description: Returns the compiled rule-sets and original DSL source for a single policy.

put.description: Upserts a single policy by name. Fails when `FIREFIK_POLICIES_READONLY=true`.

post.description: Compiles a policy (existing or supplied via `dsl`) and previews the resulting rule-sets, optionally against a container's labels.

get.description: Snapshot of what firefik currently has applied in the kernel, keyed by container. Each entry includes container name, status, default policy, and per-rule-set details.
Returns the set of built-in rule-set profiles that labels can reference (`firefik.rules.<name>.profile=<profile>`).
Returns rule templates loaded from `FIREFIK_TEMPLATES_FILE`. Each template has a deterministic 16-hex version derived from its canonical content.
Container counts (total/running/enabled) and 60 one-minute traffic buckets (`accepted`/`dropped`).
Proxies GET /v1/templates on the configured control-plane.

post.description: Proxies POST /v1/templates on the configured control-plane.

get.description: Proxies GET /v1/templates/{name} on the configured control-plane.
Returns the embedded `swagger.json` generated by swaggo. Unauthenticated.
Returns the embedded `swagger.yaml` generated by swaggo. Unauthenticated.
Always returns 200 while firefik is serving. Reports the build version.
Returns 200 when firefik can talk to Docker, 503 otherwise.
WebSocket endpoint. Frames are JSON `{event,…}` objects (see backend/internal/logstream) and periodic `{event:"dropped",count:N}` control messages when a slow consumer lags the hub.
get.summary: Subscribe to the live event stream

parameters.description: substring filter applied to each frame before send
parameters.name: filter
